element_choice.justification: Every element is the best semantic match: <details>/<summary> for expandable questions, <search> for the search landmark, <a href='tel:'> for the phone number, <a> for the contact link, <nav> for breadcrumb, <header>/<main>/<footer> for page structure. No <div> or <span> used where a semantic element exists. No <dl> misuse for FAQ items, no <address> misuse for the support section.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used only where needed: aria-label on <nav> and <search> landmarks (both required), aria-labelledby on all <section> elements, aria-current='page' on the breadcrumb current item. No redundant role attributes, no aria patching non-semantic markup. tabindex='-1' on <main> is correct for skip-link targeting.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Exactly one <h1> ('Frequently Asked Questions'), three <h2> category headings, and <h2> for 'Still need help?' — all at the same document level as the categories. No levels skipped, no headings used decoratively. Individual questions correctly use <summary> rather than <h3>.

landmark_structure.justification: All key landmarks present and correct: <header> and <footer> outside <main>, <nav aria-label='Breadcrumb'> between header and main, <main id='main-content'> wrapping only page-unique content, <search aria-label='Search FAQ'>, and labeled <section> elements for each category and the help section. Two skip links ('Skip to main content' and 'Skip to search') appear as the first focusable elements, appropriate for this page's complexity.

list_semantics.justification: Breadcrumb correctly uses <ol> (ordered, position matters). FAQ items per category use <ul>. The plan comparison nested inside a FAQ answer also uses <ul>. No list misuse or missed opportunities.

content_realism.justification: Fully realistic: named product (FlowBoard), plausible SaaS pricing tiers with real dollar figures, named integrations (Slack, GitHub, Figma, Google Drive), concrete UI paths (Settings → Members), realistic email (billing@flowboard.io) and phone number. No Lorem ipsum or placeholder content.

expectations_met: <main> wraps only the FAQ content — site header, breadcrumb nav, and footer are outside it
Two skip links present as first focusable elements: 'Skip to main content' and 'Skip to search'
Individual questions use <details>/<summary> for progressive disclosure
Questions are NOT marked up as <dl>
Native <details>/<summary> used — no custom ARIA disclosure pattern
Search/filter input has a visible <label> ('Search questions')
Contact link is an <a href='/support/contact'>, phone number is <a href='tel:+18005551234'>
No <address> element used for the support section (correctly omitted)
additional_observations: Good use of the <search> landmark element (not just a plain <div>) for the search area
sections for FAQ categories use aria-labelledby correctly, making them proper landmarks
The breadcrumb nav has inline styles alongside a CSS class — a minor style inconsistency but no semantic impact
tabindex='-1' on <main> is the correct pattern for programmatic focus from skip links
